title: Liongard Recipe
description: Schema validating the YAML frontmatter of every recipe in recipes/. Body content (markdown) is validated by the companion validator script — JSON Schema only covers the frontmatter and the primitives[] reference array.

@id.description: Stable namespaced identifier the rest of the graph references this recipe by. Format: recipe:{category}:{kebab-case-slug}. Required once the recipe is in the governance layer (migration: warning during the transition window, hard requirement after).

@type.description: Maps to a class in ontology/context.jsonld. Mirrors recipeType but written in JSON-LD form for SPARQL traversal.

name.description: Kebab-case slug. Matches the filename stem and the skill name an MCP client would invoke.

description.description: Trigger phrases + what the recipe produces + which MCP tools it calls. The recipe-picker skill matches user intent against this. Lead with 'Use this skill when...'.

compatibility.description: MCP-tool requirement statement, e.g., 'Requires Liongard MCP: liongard_environment, liongard_system, liongard_metric, liongard_asset'.

personas.description: MSP roles this recipe is designed for. Empty array allowed for cross-persona recipes.

output_formats.description: Deliverable formats this recipe knows how to produce. markdown is always supported by default.

recipeType.description: Which JSON-LD class this recipe instantiates. Drives the dependency-graph traversal in the governance layer.

evaluates.description: Required for SystemAssessmentRecipe (the single inspector this recipe targets). Optional for cross-inspector recipes.

primitives.description: ATOMS LAYER. Explicit list of MetricPrimitive @ids this recipe references. Used by the validator to verify every reference resolves to a registry entry. The body of the recipe may inline additional JMESPath that hasn't been promoted to a primitive yet — those stay recipe-local. A metric earns a registry entry when ≥ 2 recipes reference it OR it's used in a compliance/QBR organism.

composes.description: MOLECULES → ORGANISMS (downward). Recipe @ids this recipe composes — children it stitches together. Declared by the parent. Example: a QBR composes [recipe:domain:identity-posture, recipe:domain:endpoint-posture, ...]. Validator verifies every entry resolves to a real recipe file.

isPartOf.description: MOLECULES → ORGANISMS (upward). Recipe @ids this recipe is composed BY — parents that stitch it in. Inverse of `composes`. Declared by the child. Optional: the validator builds the reverse index from `composes` declarations anyway, but explicit `isPartOf` is fine when authoring bottom-up.

dependsOn.description: Recipe @ids that must run BEFORE this one (execution-order dependency, not just composition). Validator detects cycles in this graph and fails on any cycle.

isBasedOn.description: @ids of metrics, recipes, OR external sources this recipe is conceptually based on. Mixed-target — accepts metrics:* (metric primitive), recipe:* (molecule/organism), framework:* (e.g., framework:cis-controls-v8:5.1), or doc:* (e.g., doc:partner-qa-matrix).

answersQuestion.description: Cyber-insurance / CIS / onboarding questions this recipe answers. The MSP recipe-picker uses these to route 'help me answer X' prompts.

compliesWith.description: Compliance frameworks the recipe maps to.

version.description: SemVer. Bump major when a primitive reference changes incompatibly; minor when adding sections; patch for fixes.

then.description: Single-system recipes must declare the inspector they evaluate against.
